title: Requirements for Licensing under the 2002 POEA Rules on Overseas Employment of OFWs
content: The requirements for licensing include: 1) Certified copy of Articles of Incorporation or Partnership registered with SEC, or Certificate of Registration with DTI for single proprietorships. 2) Proof of financial capacity: For single proprietorship or partnership, verified income tax returns for past two years and savings account certificate with at least P500,000 balance plus authority to examine bank deposit; for newly organized corporations, savings account certificate with at least P500,000 balance and authority to examine; for existing corporations, verified financial statements, corporate tax returns for past two years, and savings account certificate with at least P500,000 balance and authority to examine. 3) Proof of marketing capability including a Special Power of Attorney or Recruitment/Service Agreement, manpower requests or visa certifications for at least 100 workers, and certification from POEA Pre-Employment Services Office on new market existence. 4) Clearances from NBI and other government agencies for all board members, partners, or proprietors; foreign members require clearance from country of origin. 5) Verified undertaking by applicant to select qualified recruits, assume full responsibility for claims and liabilities, guarantee compliance with labor and social legislations, assume responsibility for acts of officials and representatives, negotiate best employment terms, disclose full terms to workers, deploy at least 100 workers within one year, provide orientation and facilities, and repatriate workers and belongings when needed. 6) For corporations or partnerships, verified undertaking by officers, directors, partners to be jointly and severally liable for claims arising from employer-employee relationship. 7) Contract of lease or proof of building ownership with office space of at least 100 square meters. 8) Individual income tax returns for past two years of proprietor, partners, stockholders/incorporators. 9) Proof of bachelor's degree and three years business experience by sole proprietor, partner or chief executive officer. 10) List of officials and personnel involved in recruitment and placement with appointments, bio-data, passport-size pictures, and clearances from NBI and Anti-Illegal Recruitment Branch. 11) Proof of publication of notice of application with names of proprietor, partners, incorporators, and officers. 12) Certificate of attendance of owner and/or chief executive officer in a pre-application seminar conducted by the Administration.
